data_window_note: MMS L2 data ~90 days behind real-time

unusual_events.explanation: Unusually large magnetic field: B_max = 1289.4 nT (4.2σ above mean). Possible causes: magnetospheric compression, flux tube boundary, current sheet crossing, or reconnection inflow region.

electron_holes.note: 0 bipolar E∥ events above 0.83 mV/m — no clear bipolar signatures in this window

electrons.beam_note: No clear beam — mean Ve=142.9 km/s < 300 km/s threshold.
electrons.dfdv_note: v_beam/v_th = 0.01 < 1.5 → no bump-on-tail. Beam insufficient for classical TSI.

theory_validation.validation_note: Beam velocity too low for classical TSI at observed conditions.

tsi_weibel_chain.note: Chain not detected — no electron beam present

langmuir_proxy.note: f_pe=4.69 kHz outside EDP frequency range (Nyquist ~16 Hz for slow survey)
